docs.title: Route Mounting
docs.text:
Alle Routes werden unter /api/v1 gemounted (app.include_router(router, prefix="/api/v1")):

Route File Prefix Resultierende URL health.py (none) /api/v1/health, /api/v1/ready auth.py /auth /api/v1/auth/me, /api/v1/auth/publisher packages.py /packages /api/v1/packages, /api/v1/packages/{name} artifacts.py /artifacts /api/v1/artifacts, /api/v1/artifacts/{name} publishers.py /publishers /api/v1/publishers, /api/v1/publishers/verify search.py /search /api/v1/search, /api/v1/search/suggest resolve.py /resolve /api/v1/resolve, /api/v1/resolve/check versions.py /versions /api/v1/versions diff.py /api/v1 /api/v1/api/v1/packages/{name}/diff/...

Hinweis: diff.py hat prefix /api/v1 im Router, was zu doppeltem Prefix führt. Dies sollte zu /diff oder leerem Prefix korrigiert werden.

docs.title: Lokale Entwicklung
docs.text:
# Repository klonen
git clone https://github.com/MarkinHaus/ToolBoxV2.git
cd ToolBoxV2/tb-registry

# Virtuelle Umgebung
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate

# Dependencies installieren
pip install -e ".[dev]"

# Umgebung konfigurieren
cp .env.example .env
# CLOUDM_JWT_SECRET=your_secret_here

# Datenbank initialisieren
python -c "from registry.db.database import Database; import asyncio; asyncio.run(Database('sqlite:///./data/registry.db').initialize())"

# Server starten
uvicorn registry.app:app --reload --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4025

docs.title: Erster Admin (Bootstrap)
docs.text:
Der erste Admin wird direkt auf dem Server über das Admin-CLI erstellt. Dies ist der einzige Weg einen Admin zu erstellen — es gibt keinen HTTP-Endpoint dafür.

# Auf dem Registry-Server ausführen
python admin_cli.py --db ./data/registry.db

Voraussetzung: Der User muss sich mindestens einmal eingeloggt haben (wird automatisch bei erstem JWT-Login erstellt via get_current_user in deps.py).

docs.title: Admin-CLI Befehle
docs.text:
Das Admin-CLI ist ein interaktives Menü:

# Befehl Beschreibung 1 List users Alle User anzeigen 2 List publishers Alle Publisher anzeigen 3 Make publisher User zum Publisher machen + optional sofort verifizieren 4 Remove publisher Publisher-Status entfernen 5 Edit publisher Publisher-Felder bearbeiten 6 Set publisher status Verification-Status ändern (unverified/pending/verified/rejected/suspended) 7 Toggle admin Admin-Rechte an/aus 8 Raw SQL Direkte SQL-Queries (read-only, !-Prefix für write)

docs.title: Admin über API (nach Bootstrap)
docs.text:
Sobald ein Admin existiert, kann dieser über die Registry-CLI Publisher verwalten:

# Pending Publisher auflisten
tb registry admin publisher list --status pending

# Oder nur offene Requests
tb registry admin publisher open

# Publisher verifizieren
tb registry admin publisher verify --target <publisher-id>

# Publisher ablehnen
tb registry admin publisher reject --target <publisher-id> --notes "Reason"

# Verification widerrufen
tb registry admin publisher revoke --target <publisher-id>

docs.title: Admin API Endpoints
docs.text:
Endpoint Beschreibung GET /api/v1/packages/admin/pending Pending Publisher auflisten POST /api/v1/packages/admin/{publisher_id}/verify Publisher verifizieren POST /api/v1/packages/admin/{publisher_id}/reject Publisher ablehnen POST /api/v1/packages/admin/{publisher_id}/revoke Verification widerrufen

Alle Admin-Endpoints erfordern is_admin = true auf dem User.

docs.title: API-Referenz
docs.text: Siehe API Reference für vollständige Endpoint-Dokumentation.

docs.title: JWT Token erstellen (für Tests)
docs.text:
import jwt
import time

payload = {
    "user_id": "usr_test123",
    "username": "testuser",
    "email": "test@example.com",
    "level": 1,
    "provider": "magic_link",
    "exp": int(time.time()) + 3600,
    "iat": int(time.time()),
    "jti": f"jti_{int(time.time())}",
}
token = jwt.encode(payload, "your_jwt_secret", algorithm="HS256")

docs.title: Debug-Mode
docs.text:
Bei DEBUG=True und ohne konfiguriertem JWT-Secret gibt verify_cloudm_token einen Mock-User zurück:

TokenPayload(
    user_id="user_debug",
    username="debug_user",
    email="debug@example.com",
    level=1,
    provider="debug",
)

docs.title: Users Table
docs.text:
CREATE TABLE users (
    cloudm_user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    email TEXT NOT NULL,
    username TEXT NOT NULL,
    publisher_id TEXT REFERENCES publishers(id),
    is_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0,
    last_login TIMESTAMP,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

docs.title: Publishers Table
docs.text:
CREATE TABLE publishers (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    cloudm_user_id TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    slug TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    email TEXT NOT NULL,
    website TEXT,
    github TEXT,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'unverified',
    verified_at TIMESTAMP,
    verified_by TEXT,
    verification_notes TEXT,
    can_publish_public BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0,
    can_publish_artifacts BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0,
    max_package_size_mb INTEGER DEFAULT 100,
    packages_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    total_downloads INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

docs.title: Packages Table
docs.text:
CREATE TABLE packages (
    name TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    package_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    owner_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    publisher_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES publishers(id),
    visibility TEXT DEFAULT 'public',
    description TEXT DEFAULT '',
    readme TEXT DEFAULT '',
    homepage TEXT,
    repository TEXT,
    license TEXT,
    keywords TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
    latest_version TEXT,
    total_downloads INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

docs.title: Package Versions Table
docs.text:
CREATE TABLE package_versions (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    package_name TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES packages(name) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    version TEXT NOT NULL,
    released_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    changelog TEXT DEFAULT '',
    dependencies TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
    toolbox_version TEXT,
    yanked BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0,
    yank_reason TEXT,
    UNIQUE(package_name, version)
);

docs.title: Enums im Code
docs.text:
PackageType: mod, artifact, library, theme, plugin

ArtifactType: tauri_app, cli_executable, browser_extension, mobile_app, library

Visibility: public, private, unlisted

VerificationStatus: unverified, pending, verified, rejected, suspended

Platform: all, windows, linux, macos, android, ios

Architecture: all, x64, x86, arm64, arm32

docs.title: CloudM.Auth Integration
docs.text:
1. Client bekommt Token von CloudM.Auth (ToolBox Hauptanwendung)
  2. Client sendet Token an Registry im Authorization: Bearer Header
  3. Registry validiert Token lokal mit CLOUDM_JWT_SECRET
  4. User-Erststellung: Bei erstem Login wird automatisch ein User in der DB erstellt (deps.py: get_current_user)

docs.title: JWT Token Struktur
docs.text:
{
  "user_id": "usr_abc123",
  "username": "johndoe",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "level": 1,
  "provider": "discord",
  "exp": 1740451200,
  "iat": 1740447600,
  "jti": "jti_usr_abc123_1740447600"
}

docs.title: Unit Tests
docs.text:
# Alle Tests (unittest, nicht pytest!)
python -m unittest discover tests/ -v

# Spezifisches Modul
python -m unittest tests.test_packages -v

docs.title: Integration Tests
docs.text: python -m unittest discover tests/integration/ -v

docs.title: Production Setup
docs.text:
1. Environment Variables 

    CLOUDM_JWT_SECRET=<strong-random-secret>
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./data/registry.db
MINIO_PRIMARY_ENDPOINT=minio.simplecore.app
MINIO_PRIMARY_ACCESS_KEY=access_key
MINIO_PRIMARY_SECRET_KEY=secret_key
DEBUG=False

  2. Start Application 

    uvicorn registry.app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 4025 --workers 4

  3. Admin Bootstrap 

    # Erster Admin muss auf dem Server erstellt werden
python admin_cli.py --db ./data/registry.db
# → Option 7: Toggle admin für deinen User

docs.title: "Authentication service not configured"
docs.text:
# CLOUDM_JWT_SECRET nicht gesetzt
export CLOUDM_JWT_SECRET=your_secret

docs.title: "Admin access required" (403)
docs.text:
# User ist kein Admin
# Auf dem Server: python admin_cli.py → Option 7

docs.title: Diff-Route doppelter Prefix
docs.text:
Die diff.py Route ist mit prefix /api/v1 im Router gemounted, was zu /api/v1/api/v1/packages/... führt. Fix: Prefix in router.py zu leerem String ändern oder die Pfade in diff.py anpassen.

docs.text: Version: 1.1 Stand: 2026-04-28

1. Über das Registry

  4. Registry CLI

  6. Publishing Kurzanleitung
  7. Troubleshooting

docs.title: Über das Registry
docs.text:
Die ToolBoxV2 Registry ist die zentrale Verwaltungsplattform für alle ToolBoxV2 Module (Mods). Hier findest du:

  • Mods: Erweiterungen für die ToolBoxV2
  • Libraries: Nützliche Code-Bibliotheken
  • Artifacts: Kompilierte Binaries und Apps (SimpleCore Desktop, TB CLI)

docs.title: Voraussetzungen
docs.text:
# ToolBoxV2 muss installiert sein
pip install toolboxv2
tb --version

docs.title: Einloggen (für Contributors)
docs.text:
# Login über CloudM.Auth
tb registry login

docs.title: Module verwalten (CloudM)
docs.text: Die primäre Art Module zu installieren und verwalten ist über das CloudM-Modul.

docs.title: Interaktiver Manager (empfohlen)
docs.text: tb -c CloudM mods manager

docs.title: Module installieren
docs.text:
# Über CloudM
tb -c CloudM mods install <module-name>

# Shortcut
tb --install <module-name>

docs.title: Module auflisten
docs.text: tb -c CloudM mods list

docs.title: Module aktualisieren
docs.text: tb --update <module-name>

docs.title: Module entfernen
docs.text: tb --remove <module-name>

docs.title: Config generieren (für Publishing)
docs.text: tb -c CloudM mods gen-config <module-name>

docs.title: Registry CLI
docs.text: Die Registry-CLI bietet direkten Zugriff auf die Registry-API.

docs.title: Mods suchen
docs.text:
# Suche nach Mods
tb registry search discord

# Alle Mods auflisten
tb registry list

# Nach Typ filtern und sortieren
tb registry list --type mod --sort downloads --limit 20

docs.title: Mod-Details anzeigen
docs.text:
# Mod-Infos
tb registry info CloudM

# Mit Versionshistorie
tb registry info CloudM --versions

docs.title: Mod herunterladen
docs.text:
# Neueste Version herunterladen
tb registry download CloudM

# Spezifische Version
tb registry download CloudM --version 2.0.0

# In bestimmten Ordner
tb registry download CloudM --output ./mods/

docs.title: Versionen auflisten
docs.text: tb registry versions CloudM

docs.title: Aktueller User
docs.text:
# Anzeigen wer eingeloggt ist
tb registry whoami

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text:
# Registry-Status prüfen
tb registry health

docs.title: Sichtbarkeitsstufen
docs.text: Stufe Beschreibung Zugriff Public Öffentlich sichtbar Jeder kann downloaden Unlisted Nicht gelistet Nur mit direktem Link/Namen Private Privat Nur der Owner

docs.title: Public Mods
docs.text:
# Öffentliche Mods sind in der Suche sichtbar
tb registry search utility

# Jeder kann sie herunterladen
tb registry download MyUtilityMod

docs.title: Unlisted Mods
docs.text:
# Nicht in der Suche gelistet
# Aber herunterladen mit Name funktioniert:
tb registry download MyUnlistedMod

docs.title: Private Mods
docs.text:
# Private Mods benötigen Authentifizierung
tb registry login

# Download nur für den Owner
tb registry download MyPrivateMod
# Andere bekommen: 403 Forbidden

docs.title: 1. Einloggen
docs.text: tb registry login

docs.title: 2. Publisher werden
docs.text:
Ein Publisher-Account wird über die API erstellt:

# Via HTTP API
curl -X POST https://registry.simplecore.app/api/v1/auth/register-publisher \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "my-publisher",
    "display_name": "My Publisher",
    "email": "contact@example.com"
  }'

Oder über den interaktiven Manager:

tb -c CloudM mods manager
# → REGISTRY → Register as Publisher

docs.title: 3. Package erstellen und hochladen
docs.text:
# metadata.json erstellen:
# {
#   "name": "my-mod",
#   "display_name": "My Awesome Mod",
#   "package_type": "mod",
#   "version": "1.0.0",
#   "description": "What my mod does",
#   "visibility": "unlisted"
# }

# Package erstellen
tb registry publish ./my-mod --create --metadata metadata.json

# Version hochladen
tb registry publish ./my-mod --upload --metadata metadata.json

docs.title: 4. Sichtbarkeit festlegen
docs.text:
# Auf Public (erfordert verifizierten Publisher)
tb registry publish my-mod --visibility public

# Auf Unlisted (für Beta/Testing)
tb registry publish my-mod --visibility unlisted

# Auf Private
tb registry publish my-mod --visibility private

docs.title: 5. Verification beantragen
docs.text:
Für Public-Mods muss der Publisher verifiziert sein:

# Via HTTP API
curl -X POST https://registry.simplecore.app/api/v1/publishers/verify \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"method": "github", "data": {"username": "dein-github"}}'

Ein Admin prüft den Antrag und verifiziert den Publisher.

docs.title: 6. Mod verwalten
docs.text:
# Mod löschen (Vorsicht!)
tb registry delete my-mod

# Version zurückziehen
tb registry yank my-mod 1.0.0 --reason "Critical bug"

docs.title: Authentifizierungsprobleme
docs.text:
# Nicht eingeloggt?
# Error: Authentication required
# Lösung:
tb registry login

# Token abgelaufen?
# Error: Token expired / 401
# Lösung:
tb registry login

docs.title: Download-Probleme
docs.text:
# Mod nicht gefunden?
# Error: Package not found
# Lösung: Name prüfen (case-sensitive!)
tb registry search mod-name

# Version nicht gefunden?
# Error: Version not found
# Lösung: Verfügbare Versionen prüfen
tb registry versions mod-name

docs.title: Wie werde ich Publisher?
docs.text:
1. Bei der Registry einloggen: tb registry login
  2. Publisher registrieren via API (POST /api/v1/auth/register-publisher)
  3. Optional: Verification beantragen für Public-Mods

docs.title: Was ist der Unterschied zwischen Public und Unlisted?
docs.text: Public Unlisted In Suche sichtbar Nicht in Suche Jeder kann downloaden Jeder mit Name kann downloaden Verification benötigt Keine Verification nötig

docs.title: Kann ich meinen Mod später von Private auf Public ändern?
docs.text:
Ja: 

tb registry publish my-mod --visibility public

(Erfordert verifizierten Publisher.)

docs.title: Was ist der Unterschied zwischen tb registry und tb -c CloudM mods?
docs.text:
• tb -c CloudM mods — installiert/verwaltet Module lokal auf deinem System
  • tb registry — interagiert direkt mit der Registry-API (suchen, hochladen, herunterladen)

Für alltägliches Modmanagement nutze tb -c CloudM mods manager. Für Publishing und Registry-Verwaltung nutze tb registry.

docs.text:
• Contributors Guide - Ausführliche Anleitung für Contributors
  • Developers Guide - Für System-Entwickler
  • API Reference - HTTP-API Endpunkte
  • GitHub Repository - Source Code

Letzte Aktualisierung: 2026-04-28 Kontakt: support@toolboxv2.app

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Blob Watch API enables real-time monitoring of blob changes with automatic callback execution. It provides a non-blocking, thread-safe way to react to blob updates.

docs.title: Features
docs.text: ✅ Non-Blocking: Runs in background thread, doesn't block your application ✅ Batch Updates: Multiple watches run in a single thread for efficiency ✅ Auto-Cleanup: Callbacks auto-remove after configurable idle timeout ✅ Thread-Safe: Safe to use from multiple threads ✅ Easy to Use: Simple callback-based API

docs.title: BlobStorage.watch()
docs.text:
Register a callback for blob changes.

def watch(
    blob_id: str,
    callback: Callable[[BlobFile], None],
    max_idle_timeout: int = 600,
    threaded: bool = True
)

Parameters: - blob_id (str): The blob to watch for changes - callback (Callable): Function called when blob changes, receives BlobFile object - max_idle_timeout (int): Seconds without updates before auto-removing callback (default: 600 = 10 min) - threaded (bool): If True, runs in background thread (default: True)

Example: 

def on_change(blob_file: BlobFile):
    data = blob_file.read_json()
    print(f"Updated: {data}")

storage.watch('app/config.json', on_change, max_idle_timeout=600)

docs.title: BlobStorage.stop_watch()
docs.text:
Stop watching a blob.

def stop_watch(
    blob_id: str,
    callback: Optional[Callable] = None
)

Parameters: - blob_id (str): The blob to stop watching - callback (Optional[Callable]): Specific callback to remove, or None to remove all

Examples: 

# Stop specific callback
storage.stop_watch('app/config.json', on_change)

# Stop all callbacks for a blob
storage.stop_watch('app/config.json')

docs.title: BlobStorage.stop_all_watches()
docs.text:
Stop all active watches and shutdown the watch thread.

def stop_all_watches()

Example: 

# Cleanup on application shutdown
storage.stop_all_watches()

docs.title: BlobFile.watch()
docs.text:
Convenience method to watch a specific BlobFile instance.

def watch(
    callback: Callable[[BlobFile], None],
    max_idle_timeout: int = 600,
    threaded: bool = True
)

Example: 

config_file = BlobFile('app/config.json', 'r')

def on_change(blob_file: BlobFile):
    print("Config changed!")

config_file.watch(on_change)

docs.title: BlobFile.stop_watch()
docs.text:
Stop watching this BlobFile instance.

def stop_watch(callback: Optional[Callable] = None)

Example: 

config_file.stop_watch(on_change)

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
1. WatchManager: Manages all watch operations in a background thread
  2. Long-Polling: Uses server's /watch endpoint (60s timeout)
  3. Batch Processing: One thread handles all watches efficiently
  4. Callback Dispatch: When blob changes, all registered callbacks are called
  5. Auto-Cleanup: Callbacks without updates for max_idle_timeout are removed

docs.title: Flow Diagram
docs.text:
┌─────────────┐
│ Application │
└──────┬──────┘
       │ watch(blob_id, callback)
       ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│  WatchManager   │◄─── Background Thread
└────────┬────────┘
         │ Long-Polling /watch
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│  Blob Server    │
└────────┬────────┘
         │ blob_id changed
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Dispatch        │
│ Callbacks       │
└────────┬────────┘
         │ callback(BlobFile)
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Your Callback   │
└─────────────────┘

docs.title: 1. Use Appropriate Timeouts
docs.text:
# Short-lived watches (e.g., UI updates)
storage.watch('ui/state.json', on_ui_change, max_idle_timeout=60)

# Long-lived watches (e.g., config monitoring)
storage.watch('app/config.json', on_config_change, max_idle_timeout=3600)

docs.title: 2. Handle Errors in Callbacks
docs.text:
def safe_callback(blob_file: BlobFile):
    try:
        data = blob_file.read_json()
        process_data(data)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Callback error: {e}")

docs.title: 3. Cleanup on Shutdown
docs.text:
import atexit

storage = BlobStorage(servers=['http://localhost:3000'])
atexit.register(storage.stop_all_watches)

docs.title: 4. Multiple Callbacks per Blob
docs.text:
# You can register multiple callbacks for the same blob
storage.watch('data.json', callback_1, max_idle_timeout=300)
storage.watch('data.json', callback_2, max_idle_timeout=600)

# Each callback has independent timeout

docs.title: Performance Considerations
docs.text:
• Single Thread: All watches run in one thread, very efficient
  • Batch Updates: Server sends one notification, all callbacks triggered
  • Memory: Minimal overhead, only stores callback references
  • Network: One long-polling connection per server

docs.title: Callback Not Called
docs.text:
1. Check if blob is actually changing on server
  2. Verify API key is valid
  3. Check logs for errors
  4. Ensure watch wasn't auto-removed due to timeout

docs.title: High CPU Usage
docs.text:
• Should not happen - watch thread sleeps during long-polling
  • Check for errors in callbacks causing rapid retries

docs.title: Memory Leaks
docs.text:
• Always call stop_watch() or stop_all_watches() when done
  • Use max_idle_timeout to auto-cleanup stale watches

docs.title: BlobDB Reference
docs.text: Server-Blob-Datenbank basierend auf MinIO für skalierbaren Storage.

docs.title: BlobDB Overview
docs.text: BlobDB ist eine Key-Value-Datenbank für Server-Daten mit MinIO-Backend.

docs.title: Kern-Features
docs.text: Feature Beschreibung MinIO Backend Lokaler MinIO + optionaler Cloud Sync Offline-Fallback SQLite bei keiner MinIO-Verbindung Cache mit TTL Konfigurierbares Caching Manifest-Tracking Schnelle Key-Suche ohne Scan

docs.title: Aktivierung
docs.text: export IS_OFFLINE_DB=true

docs.title: SQLite Schema
docs.text:
CREATE TABLE blobs (
    path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    data BLOB NOT NULL,
    checksum TEXT,
    updated_at REAL,
    sync_status TEXT DEFAULT 'dirty'
);

CREATE TABLE manifest (
    key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    created_at REAL
);

docs.title: Sync-Status
docs.text: Status Bedeutung dirty Geändert, nicht gesynct synced Mit Cloud synchronisiert

docs.title: When to use BlobDB vs LC
Local Dictionary (LC) - Wann?
docs.text:
Kriterium Empfehlung Datenvolumen < 10 MB Zugriffe < 100/s Verteilung Single-Instance Komplexität Einfache Key-Value

Typische Use-Cases: - User Settings - Mod-Konfiguration - Lokale Caches

docs.title: BlobDB (MinIO) - Wann?
docs.text:
Kriterium Empfehlung Datenvolumen > 10 MB bis TBs Zugriffe > 100/s Verteilung Multi-Instance / Cluster Komplexität Enterprise-Grade

Typische Use-Cases: - Server-Sessions - Binäre Assets - Audit Logs - Backup-Storage

docs.title: Lokaler MinIO
docs.text: Variable Default Pflicht MINIO_ENDPOINT 127.0.0.1:9000 Nein MINIO_ACCESS_KEY admin Nein MINIO_SECRET_KEY - Ja MINIO_SECURE false Nein

docs.title: Cloud MinIO (optional)
docs.text: Variable Pflicht CLOUD_ENDPOINT Nein CLOUD_ACCESS_KEY Nein CLOUD_SECRET_KEY Nein CLOUD_SECURE Nein

docs.title: Betrieb
docs.text: Variable Default Beschreibung IS_OFFLINE_DB false Nur SQLite, kein MinIO SERVER_ID hostname Server-Identifier DB_CACHE_TTL 60 Cache-Lebensdauer (Sekunden)

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
from toolboxv2.mods.DB.blob_instance import create_db

# Initialisierung
db = create_db("my_server")

# CRUD
db.set("users/123", {"name": "Alice"})
result = db.get("users/123")
print(result.get())  # {"name": "Alice"}

# Pattern Matching
db.set("config/theme", "dark")
results = db.get("config/*")

# Cloud Sync
db.sync_to_cloud()

# Cleanup
db.exit()

docs.title: Bucket & Path Structure
docs.text:
Bucket: tb-servers
│
└── {SERVER_ID}/
    ├── _manifest.json      # Key-Index
    ├── users/
    │   └── 123.json
    ├── config/
    │   └── theme.json
    └── ...

docs.title: ToolBoxV2 Database Modes Reference
docs.text: Dokumentation der unterstützten Datenbank-Modi für ToolBoxV2.

docs.title: DatabaseModes Enum
docs.text:
Definiert die verfügbaren Datenbank-Modi.

Modus Wert Beschreibung LC LOCAL_DICT Lokale JSON-Datei (Standard) LR LOCAL_REDIS Lokaler Redis-Server RR REMOTE_REDIS Remote Redis-Server CB CLUSTER_BLOB MinIO/S3 Blob Storage

docs.title: DatabaseModes.crate(mode: str) -> DatabaseModes
docs.text:
Erstellt eine DatabaseModes-Instanz aus einem String.

from toolboxv2.mods.DB.types import DatabaseModes

mode = DatabaseModes.crate("LC")  # -> DatabaseModes.LC

docs.title: AuthenticationTypes Enum
docs.text:
Definiert die Authentifizierungstypen für Datenbankverbindungen.

Typ Wert Verwendung UserNamePassword password Klassische Benutzer/Passwort-Auth Uri url Connection-String in URI-Form PassKey passkey Auth via Passkey location location Dateibasierte Auth (Standard für LC) none none Keine Auth erforderlich

docs.title: tb-manifest.yaml Beispiel
docs.text:
database:
  mode: LC  # oder LR, RR, CB

  local:
    path: ".data/MiniDictDB.json"

  redis:
    url: "${DB_CONNECTION_URI:redis://localhost:6379}"
    username: "${DB_USERNAME:}"
    password: "${DB_PASSWORD:}"

  minio:
    endpoint: "${MINIO_ENDPOINT:localhost:9000}"
    access_key: "${MINIO_ACCESS_KEY:minioadmin}"
    secret_key: "${MINIO_SECRET_KEY:minioadmin}"
    bucket: user-data-enc

docs.title: tb_browser — Chrome Extension
docs.text:
Location: toolboxv2/tb_browser/ Build: npm run dev / npm run build Backend: ToolBoxV2 local server on localhost:8080

A production-ready browser extension integrating the full ToolBoxV2 stack into Chrome. Provides gesture control, ISAA AI assistant, voice input, live page search, and a password manager with 2FA.

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Feature Description 🎯 Gesture Control Mouse/touch swipe navigation (back, forward, scroll) 🤖 ISAA AI Assistant Page-aware AI chat, web automation, form filling 🎤 Voice Input Speech-to-text, text-to-speech, wake words 🔍 Live Search Auto-index page content, in-page highlighting, section jump 🔐 Password Manager Auto-fill, TOTP 2FA, browser import, secure generation 🎨 Glass Morphism UI Black/white glass look, marine blue accents (#2E86AB)

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
tb_browser/
├── src/
│   ├── popup.html          ← Main popup (three-panel: ISAA / Search / Passwords)
│   ├── popup.js            ← UI management + panel switching
│   ├── popup.css           ← Glass morphism styling
│   ├── content.js          ← Page indexing, highlighting, gesture detection
│   ├── content.css         ← Content script styling
│   ├── background.js       ← Service worker, API communication
│   └── gesture-detector.js ← Swipe/touch recognition
├── icons/
├── assets/
├── build/                  ← Output directory (load this in Chrome)
├── manifest.json           ← Extension manifest (Manifest V3)
├── package.json
└── build.js

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
cd toolboxv2/tb_browser
npm run dev

Then in Chrome: chrome://extensions/ → Enable Developer Mode → Load Unpacked → select build/

docs.title: Production
docs.text:
npm run package     # Creates distributable ZIP
npm run clean       # Clean build directory

docs.title: Native Messaging Host
docs.text:
For full ToolBoxV2 integration (beyond the HTTP API), install the native messaging host:

tb -c tb_browser install_native_host
Linux / macOSWindows

Copies toolbox_native_host.json to ~/.config/google-chrome/NativeMessagingHosts/

Registers registry key: HKCU\Software\Google\Chrome\NativeMessagingHosts\com.toolboxv2.host

Disconnected Status

If the extension shows Disconnected, verify:

  1. toolbox_native_host.json has the absolute path to toolbox_native_host.py
  2. toolbox_native_host.py is executable (chmod +x)
  3. Python shebang matches your active virtualenv

docs.title: Native Messaging Protocol
docs.text:
4-byte little-endian length prefix + JSON:

[4 bytes LE uint32: message_length][JSON bytes]

Example message:

{"type": "run_command", "mod": "CloudM", "fn": "session_status", "args": {}}

docs.title: API Integration
docs.text:
The extension connects to localhost:8080. Required endpoints:

Endpoint Purpose /api/isaa/mini_task_completion ISAA AI processing /api/call/PasswordManager/list_passwords List passwords /api/call/PasswordManager/generate_password Generate password /api/call/PasswordManager/get_password_for_autofill Auto-fill data /api/system/status Connection status check

docs.title: Gesture Configuration
docs.text:
{
  enabled: true,
  sensitivity: 1.0,
  minSwipeDistance: 100,
  enableMouse: true,
  enableTouch: true
}
Gesture Action Swipe Left Browser back Swipe Right Browser forward Swipe Up Scroll up (length-dependent speed) Double-Click Open popup (focus search)

docs.title: Voice Configuration
docs.text:
{
  enabled: true,
  language: 'en-US',
  autoSpeak: false,
  wakeWords: ['toolbox', 'isaa', 'computer', 'assistant']
}

Voice commands: "Search [query]", "Password fill", or any free-form query → ISAA.

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text: Shortcut Action Alt + T Toggle popup Ctrl + Shift + V Activate voice Ctrl + Shift + F Quick page search Ctrl + Shift + P Auto-fill password

docs.title: Test Suite
docs.text:
# All 132 tests
tb -c tb_browser test

# Unit tests (105)
python -m unittest discover toolboxv2/tb_browser/tests/unit/

# E2E tests (27) — requires Chrome + ChromeDriver
python -m unittest discover toolboxv2/tb_browser/tests/e2e/

docs.title: Browser Compatibility
docs.text: Browser Min Version Notes Chrome 88+ Full support (Manifest V3) Edge 88+ Full support (Chromium) Firefox 109+ Requires manifest adaptation Safari — Not supported

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Extension not loading → Check ToolBoxV2 server is running on localhost:8080, check browser console.

Gestures not working → Verify gesture detection enabled in settings, refresh the page.

Voice not responding → Check microphone permissions, verify HTTPS context (or localhost).

Password autofill failing → Check server accessibility, verify domain entry exists.

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• ISAA Agent Framework — AI backend powering ISAA chat in the extension
  • CloudM Login System — Session tokens used for API auth
  • Worker System — Local server the extension talks to

docs.title: Adaptive Prompt System
docs.text: Generates system-optimized prompts by discovering LLM capabilities, building profiles, and injecting prompt-engineering techniques (Chain of Thought, Tree of Thoughts, Adversarial Validation). Provides both an interactive CLI and a web UI.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The module implements a two-phase adaptive prompt pipeline: Discovery sends benchmark prompts to a target LLM and analyzes responses to build a SystemProfile; Generation uses that profile to construct meta-prompts tailored to the target system's strengths, format preferences, and context window. A PromptLibrary persists templates and profiles to disk. The module is structured in three layers: data models (LLMSystem, SystemProfile, PromptTemplate), core logic (PromptLibrary, AdaptivePromptGenerator, run), and UI (rendering helpers + ui entry point).

docs.title: LLMSystem(Enum)
docs.text:
Known LLM systems with their characteristics.

Value String CLAUDE "claude" CHATGPT "chatgpt" GPT4 "gpt4" GEMINI "gemini" LLAMA "llama" MISTRAL "mistral" UNKNOWN "unknown"

docs.title: SystemProfile
docs.text:
Profil eines LLM-Systems basierend auf Discovery.

Field Type Default Description system_type LLMSystem UNKNOWN Detected LLM system model_name str "" Model name capabilities list[str] [] Detected capabilities limitations list[str] [] Detected limitations preferred_format str "markdown" Preferred output format supports_system_prompt bool True Whether system prompts are supported supports_tools bool False Whether tool use is supported supports_vision bool False Whether vision/image input is supported supports_code_execution bool False Whether code execution is supported context_window int 8000 Approximate context window in tokens strengths list[str] [] Identified strengths raw_discovery_response str "" Raw discovery output discovered_at str datetime.now().isoformat() Timestamp of discovery

docs.title: PromptTemplate
docs.text:
Template für system-spezifische Prompts.

Field Type Default Description name str — Template name description str — Template description persona str — Persona definition context_template str — Context template string format_instructions str — Output format instructions chain_of_thought bool False CoT technique flag tree_of_thoughts bool False ToT technique flag adversarial_validation bool False Adversarial validation flag few_shot_examples list[dict] [] Few-shot example pairs system_type LLMSystem UNKNOWN Target system type tags list[str] [] Searchable tags

docs.title: PromptLibrary
docs.text:
Bibliothek für system-spezifische Prompt-Templates. Speichert und verwaltet optimierte Prompts pro LLM-System.

Method Signature Description __init__ (storage_path: str = "./prompt_library") Initializes library, loads existing data from disk _load_library () → None Lädt gespeicherte Templates und Profile from JSON files save_library () → None Speichert Templates und Profile to JSON files add_template (template: PromptTemplate) → None Fügt ein neues Template hinzu get_template (name: str, system_type: LLMSystem = UNKNOWN) → PromptTemplate \| None Holt ein Template für ein bestimmtes System; falls back to generic template add_profile (name: str, profile: SystemProfile) → None Speichert ein System-Profil get_profile (name: str) → SystemProfile \| None Holt ein gespeichertes System-Profil

Storage is backed by templates.json and profiles.json in storage_path.

docs.title: AdaptivePromptGenerator
docs.text:
Hauptklasse für die 2-Way Prompt-Generierung. Flow: Discovery → Profile Creation → Prompt Adaptation.

Method Signature Description __init__ (library: PromptLibrary) Initializes with a PromptLibrary reference discover_system (isaa, target_interface: Callable[[str], str], profile_name: str = "default") → SystemProfile STEP 1: Sends benchmark prompts to the target system, analyzes responses via ISAA, saves the resulting profile _analyze_discovery (isaa, discovery_response: str) → SystemProfile Parses the combined discovery response into a structured SystemProfile using an LLM-based JSON extraction generate_adaptive_prompt (isaa, user_request: str, profile: SystemProfile \| None, use_cot: bool, use_tot: bool, use_adversarial: bool, custom_persona: str \| None, few_shot_examples: list[dict] \| None) → dict[str, str] STEP 2: Builds a meta-prompt incorporating the system profile and selected techniques, returns {"system_prompt", "user_prompt"} _build_meta_prompt (user_request, profile, use_cot, use_tot, use_adversarial, custom_persona, few_shot_examples) → str Constructs the full meta-prompt for the prompt-engineering agent

docs.title: run(app, args_sto, mode, user_request, profile_name, ...) → dict[str, Any]
docs.text:
async def run(
    app, args_sto,
    mode: str = "interactive",
    user_request: str = "",
    profile_name: str = "default",
    target_response: Optional[str] = None,
    use_cot: bool = True,
    use_tot: bool = False,
    use_adversarial: bool = False,
    custom_persona: Optional[str] = None,
    few_shot_examples: Optional[list[dict]] = None,
    storage_path: str = "./prompt_library",
    target_system: str = "claude",
    **kwargs,
) -> dict[str, Any]:

Main entry point. Supports "interactive" (CLI menu loop), "quick" (direct generation with a predefined profile), and "discover" (system discovery from a provided response).

docs.title: _generate_optimized_prompt(isaa, user_request, profile, use_cot, use_tot, use_adversarial, custom_persona) → dict[str, str]
docs.text:
async def _generate_optimized_prompt(
    isaa, user_request: str, profile: SystemProfile,
    use_cot: bool = True, use_tot: bool = False,
    use_adversarial: bool = False,
    custom_persona: Optional[str] = None,
) -> dict[str, str]:

Generiert einen optimierten Prompt mit verbessertem Meta-Prompt. Builds a German-language meta-prompt incorporating the system profile and technique instructions, dispatches it to the AdaptivePromptGenerator agent.

docs.title: _create_quick_profile(system: str) → SystemProfile
docs.text: Erstellt ein vordefiniertes Profil basierend auf bekannten System-Charakteristiken. Returns a SystemProfile pre-populated with known capabilities for "claude", "chatgpt", "gemini", "llama", "mistral", or a generic default.

docs.title: _build_technique_instructions(use_cot: bool, use_tot: bool, use_adversarial: bool) → str
docs.text: Baut Technik-Anweisungen. Returns formatted instruction strings for each enabled technique (CoT, ToT, Adversarial), or a fallback message if none are active.

docs.title: _get_instances(storage_path: str = "./prompt_library") → tuple[PromptLibrary, AdaptivePromptGenerator]
docs.text: Initialisiert oder holt globale Instanzen. Returns singleton PromptLibrary and AdaptivePromptGenerator instances (lazy-initialized on first call).

docs.title: get_discovery_prompt(prompt_type: str = "identity") → str
docs.text: Gibt einen Discovery-Prompt zurück. Looks up the prompt from the module-level DISCOVERY_PROMPTS dict.

docs.title: create_few_shot_example(input_text: str, output_text: str) → dict
docs.text: Erstellt ein Few-Shot Beispiel. Returns {"input": input_text, "output": output_text}.

docs.title: _profile_to_dict(profile: SystemProfile) → dict
docs.text: Konvertiert SystemProfile zu dict. Selects key fields (system_type, model_name, capabilities, limitations, strengths, preferred_format, context_window, supports_tools, supports_vision) for serialization.

docs.title: _print_result(result: dict, profile: SystemProfile) → None
docs.text: Gibt das Ergebnis formatiert aus. Prints the generated system prompt, user prompt, and reasoning to stdout.

docs.title: ui(view) → Component
docs.text: Custom UI für Adaptive Prompt System. Entry point for the web UI; delegates to _build_ui(view) with view context management.

docs.title: UI Rendering Helpers (Private)
docs.text:
These functions construct specific tabs and states for the web UI:

  • _build_ui(view) — Baut die eigentliche UI. Assembles all tabs, registers callbacks, and returns the layout.
  • _render_quick_mode(view, vals, generate_handler) — Quick Generate Tab with target system selector, request input, and technique checkboxes.
  • _render_discovery_mode(view, vals, step, start_handler, process_handler) — Discovery Tab with prompt type selector and instructions.
  • _render_full_flow_mode(view, vals) — Full Flow Tab combining discovery and generation.
  • _render_profiles_mode(view, switch_discovery_handler) — Profiles Tab for viewing saved profiles.
  • _render_success(view, header, vals, reset_handler) — Success State mit Ergebnis-Anzeige. Renders the generated prompt or discovered profile.
  • _technique_card(name: str, description: str, enabled: bool = False) — Rendert eine Technik-Karte. UI component for a single technique toggle.

docs.title: bg
docs.text: Background runner that initializes a ToolBoxV2 application in daemon mode, optionally starting the API server before connecting.

docs.title: Why This Matters
docs.text: When you need to run a ToolBoxV2 app as a long-lived background service, this is the entry point. It handles the startup sequence — printing status, starting the API server (in debug or production mode), and connecting the daemon — in a single async call.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
import asyncio
from toolboxv2.flows.bg import run

# Start the app with API server enabled (default)
await run(app)

docs.title: Starting with API server (default)
docs.text:
import asyncio
from toolboxv2.flows.bg import run

# Launches API server (debug or production based on app.debug)
# then connects the daemon
await run(app)

docs.title: Daemon-only mode (no API server)
docs.text:
import asyncio
from toolboxv2.flows.bg import run

# Skips API server startup, connects daemon directly
await run(app, api=False)

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
The function executes a three-step startup sequence:

  1. Prints a status message via app.print("Running...").
  2. If api is True, checks app.debug: starts the debug handler when debug mode is on, otherwise starts the production API server via manage_server("start"). Both utilities are lazily imported from toolboxv2.utils.clis.api.
  3. Connects the daemon application via await app.daemon_app.connect(app).

The conditional import keeps the module lightweight when API functionality isn't needed.

docs.title: run(app, api=True) (async)
docs.text:
Starts the background runner for a ToolBoxV2 application, optionally launching the API server before connecting the daemon.

Parameters: - app — ToolBoxV2 application instance (must have .print(), .debug, and .daemon_app attributes) - api — Whether to start the API server before daemon connection. Defaults to True. When False, skips server startup entirely.

Returns: None (async — awaits app.daemon_app.connect(app))
